body:
Title: CRA Form NU428 and Worksheet NU428 2025 Nunavut resident tax.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5014-c/5014-c-25e.txt
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5014-c/5014-c-25e.pdf
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5014-d/5014-d-25e.txt
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5014-d/5014-d-25e.pdf

NU428 numeric source values for encoded amounts, rates, thresholds, caps, and limits:
Value: 54707
Value: 109413
Value: 177881

CRA Form NU428 2025 calculates Nunavut tax for residents. Part A calculates tax on taxable income from line 26000. The bracket thresholds are $54,707, $109,413, and $177,881. The rates are 4%, 7%, 9%, and 11.5%. The base tax amounts are $0.00, $2,188.28, $6,017.70, and $12,179.82.

Part B calculates Nunavut non-refundable tax credits. Line 58040 basic personal amount is $19,274. Worksheet NU428 line 58080 age amount has a maximum of $12,303, starts reducing above $45,522, is zero at $127,542 or more, and uses a 15% reduction rate. Lines 58120 and 58160 use a base amount of $19,274. Line 58230 multiplies the number of young children by $1,200. Line 58360 pension income amount is capped at $2,000. Line 58440 disability amount for self is $16,405 unless the under-18 worksheet applies. Line 58689 medical expenses are reduced by the lesser of $2,834 and 3% of net income, then medical expenses for other dependants line 58729 are added. Line 58840 multiplies the total claim amount by 4%. Donations and gifts line 58969 multiplies federal Schedule 9 line 13 by 4% and line 14 by 11.5%. Line 61500 adds line 58840 and line 58969 as Nunavut non-refundable tax credits.

Part C calculates Nunavut tax. Line 55 adds tax on taxable income and tax on split income line 61510. Worksheet NU428 line 61520 calculates the dividend tax credit by multiplying taxable dividends other than eligible dividends line 12010 by 2.61% and taxable dividends line 12000 minus line 12010 by 5.51%. Lines 58 and 61 multiply minimum tax amounts by 45%. Line 64 subtracts territorial foreign tax credit. Line 62290 gives a $722 tax credit for volunteer firefighters and search and rescue volunteers. Line 66 enters Nunavut tax on line 42800.
